event: Sodapoppin began streaming Among Us regularly. Generally credited as the inflection point that brought the game from obscurity to mainstream Twitch attention.
xQc became the most-watched streamer on Twitch, racking up 16.9 million hours watched in 30 days, largely from Among Us. Among Us reached 143 million total hours watched in the same period.
G FUEL hosted the largest Among Us stream event yet, featuring PewDiePie, xQc, Summit1g, Logic, Lirik, MoistCritikal, and others playing together across multiple channels.

During the Fall Guys beta (July 31 to August 3), xQc and Lirik dominated viewership before Steam launch (xQc 513K hours watched, Lirik 339K hours watched in 4 days). Beta peaked at 250K concurrent viewers.

Fall Guys reached 523K peak concurrent Twitch viewers on day five post-launch. TimTheTatman's quest for his first crown drove enormous viewership numbers (peaks of 69,581 on day one of his Fall Guys streams), with Ibai and DrLupo also joining the top streamer rotation.
SplatterCatGaming discovered Vampire Survivors and posted a coverage video, the first major streamer to highlight the game.
Northernlion's first Vampire Survivors stream titled '2022's first sleeper hit'. Repeat plays over the following weeks drove the game's player count from a few hundred to over 50,000 concurrent.
Forsen began streaming Lethal Company. The Forsen audience and stream snipers brought significant viral attention to the game during the November Twitch spike.
Markiplier published 'WE LOVE THE COMPANY' with Bob and Wade, a long-form YouTube playthrough series. Drove the December Google Trends peak and helped sustain Steam player counts above 240K.
